title: Log In To or Log Out From the Hospital HMI Using the Default Screen
summary: Use the Hospital HMI Default screen to perform login or logout on the hospital HMI. The source supports that the Default screen displays installed software information and the Hospital Station IP address, and that users can log in to and log out from the hospital HMI, but it does not provide exact button labels, prompts, credentials, or confirmation messages.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a user needs to log in to or log out from the Hospital HMI from the Default screen.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to update HMI date or time.
Do not use this runbook to operate the REBOOT TO SETUP function.
Do not use this runbook to infer undocumented credential requirements, prompts, button labels, or status messages.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Use the Hospital HMI Default screen to perform login or logout based on the controls actually presented by the HMI.
Escalation point if login/logout controls are not available or the HMI does not clearly reflect the requested state.

safety_notes: Only maintenance personnel should use the REBOOT TO SETUP button when updating the HMI date or time.
Do not invent credential requirements, confirmation prompts, or status messages because the source does not provide them.
access_or_tools_needed: Any required user credentials if prompted by the HMI

system_preconditions: The Hospital HMI Default screen is available.

visual_references.description: Hospital HMI 2.4 Default screen showing installed software information, the station IP address, and login/logout access.
Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu showing access to the 2.4 Default screen.

steps.title: Open the Hospital HMI Default screen
steps.purpose: Reach the screen from which the source states login and logout can be performed.
steps.instruction: Open the Hospital HMI "Default" screen. If starting from the Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu, use the available access to the 2.4 Default screen.
steps.expected_result: The Hospital HMI Default screen is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The Default screen is visible and shows information related to the installed software and the Hospital Station IP address.
steps.failure_condition: The Default screen cannot be reached or does not display the expected Default screen information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Hospital HMI 2.4 Default screen layout, including installed software information, station IP address, and login/logout access if visible.
Maintenance Menu access to the 2.4 Default screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Default screen is not available from the Hospital HMI.
The displayed screen does not match the Default screen described by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The Hospital HMI Maintenance Menu has four buttons that allow access to a variety of information: 2.1 Manual Control, 2.2 IO Diagnostics, 2.3 Persistent Variables, and 2.4 Default.
The "Default" screen displays information related to the software currently installed and the IP address of the Hospital Station.

steps.title: Locate the login or logout control
steps.purpose: Identify the on-screen control that supports the requested session action.
steps.instruction: On the Default screen, locate the available login or logout control presented by the HMI.
steps.expected_result: A login or logout control is visible on the Default screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The Default screen presents a control that allows the user to log in to or log out from the hospital HMI.
steps.failure_condition: No login or logout control is visible on the Default screen.

actions.label: Find login/logout control
actions.exact_action: Locate the login or logout control on the Default screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Default Hospital HMI 2.4 screen showing installed software information, the station IP address, and login/logout access.

stop_or_escalate_if: The login or logout control is not available on the Default screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users can also log in to and log out from the hospital HMI.

steps.title: Perform the requested login or logout action
steps.purpose: Execute the requested HMI session change using the control provided on the Default screen.
steps.instruction: Use the available control to log in to the hospital HMI or log out from the hospital HMI, following the on-screen interaction that is actually presented. If the HMI prompts for credentials, use the required credentials available to the user.
steps.expected_result: The requested login or logout action is completed from the Default screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The HMI accepts the requested login or logout interaction without requiring undocumented steps beyond what is shown on screen.
steps.failure_condition: The HMI does not complete the requested login or logout action, or the interaction requires undocumented information that is not available to the user.

actions.label: Use login/logout control
actions.exact_action: Use the available control to log in to the hospital HMI or log out from the hospital HMI, following the on-screen interaction that is actually presented.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Default Hospital HMI 2.4 screen showing login/logout access.

stop_or_escalate_if: The HMI does not allow the requested login or logout action from the Default screen.
The interaction requires undocumented prompts or controls that cannot be completed safely from the source guidance.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users can also log in to and log out from the hospital HMI.

steps.title: Confirm the resulting logged-in or logged-out state
steps.purpose: Verify that the HMI reflects the requested session state using only source-supported on-screen indication.
steps.instruction: Confirm that the HMI reflects the requested logged-in or logged-out state using only the on-screen indication provided by the system.
steps.expected_result: The HMI visibly reflects the requested logged-in or logged-out state.
steps.healthy_condition: The on-screen state is consistent with the requested login or logout action.
steps.failure_condition: The HMI does not clearly reflect the requested state, or the displayed state appears inconsistent with the action taken.

actions.label: Verify session state
actions.exact_action: Confirm that the HMI reflects the requested logged-in or logged-out state using only the on-screen indication provided by the system.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Any on-screen indication on the Default screen that shows whether the HMI is in the requested logged-in or logged-out state.

stop_or_escalate_if: The HMI does not clearly indicate the resulting logged-in or logged-out state.
The displayed state does not match the requested action.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users can also log in to and log out from the hospital HMI.

success_criteria: The user completes a login or logout action from the Hospital HMI Default screen.
The HMI reflects the requested logged-in or logged-out state using the on-screen indication provided by the system.
healthy_conditions: The Hospital HMI Default screen is available.
The Default screen shows installed software information and the Hospital Station IP address.
A login or logout control is available on the Default screen.
The HMI reflects the requested session state after the action.
failure_conditions: The Default screen cannot be reached.
The login or logout control is not available on the Default screen.
The HMI does not complete the requested login or logout action.
The HMI does not clearly indicate the resulting logged-in or logged-out state.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the login or logout control is not available on the Default screen.
Escalate if the HMI does not clearly reflect the requested logged-in or logged-out state.
Escalate if the interaction requires undocumented credential requirements, confirmation prompts, or status messages not provided by the source.

Maintenance Menu access path to the 2.4 Default screen.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Users can also log in to and log out from the hospital HMI.

missing_details: The source does not provide the exact login control label.
The source does not provide the exact logout control label.
The source does not provide the exact navigation sequence to reach the Default screen from every starting point.
The source does not provide credential requirements or user account rules.
The source does not provide confirmation prompts or status message text for successful login or logout.
The source does not provide explicit failure messages or recovery actions beyond escalation.
